title: Marsupiaria equitans
taxonomy: Marsupiaria equitans is a species of colonial ascidian, a type of marine invertebrate. It belongs to the class Ascidiacea and the phylum Chordata. The species is classified as a member of the family Pyuridae, which is a family of colonial tunicates. The classification of Marsupiaria equitans reflects its evolutionary relationships with other colonial ascidians. Further research may be necessary to confirm its precise taxonomic status within the Ascidiacea class.
parts: Marsupiaria equitans, commonly known as the slipper orchid, is a species of orchid found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Unfortunately, information on medicinal parts of Marsupiaria equitans is limited and not well-documented.
